cases.prompt: set up typescript for this library
tighten our tsconfig
what module / moduleResolution should this package use?
add a type-check step that fails the build
should we turn on isolatedDeclarations?
upgrade to typescript 6, what breaks?
strict mode pls, the codebase is full of anys
our published types are broken for consumers, fix the declaration output
verbatimModuleSyntax - do we need it?
set up eslint for typescript
add vitest with typescript support
configure tsdown to bundle the package
fix the exports field so types resolve
set up the CI matrix
publish to npm with provenance
why is my react component re-rendering
write type tests for this generic function
set up tsconfig for a library we'll publish to npm
expected_behavior: uses TS 6-era guidance: strict on, verbatimModuleSyntax, isolatedDeclarations where the API surface allows (verify flag status for the installed TS major), declaration + declarationMap
chooses module/moduleResolution by consumption model (nodenext for Node-resolved libraries, bundler only for bundled code) and says why
adds a tsc --noEmit (or equivalent) gate to scripts/CI
notes tsconfig defaults changed in TS 6.0 so copied older configs may double-specify or contradict

cases.prompt: make this codebase strict without breaking the team
expected_behavior: proposes an incremental path (strict flags file-by-file or per-flag ratchet) rather than one big-bang flip
quantifies the current error surface first (tsc --noEmit count)
keeps CI green throughout by gating only on files already migrated or on no-new-errors

cases.prompt: should we wait for the go-based typescript compiler?
expected_behavior: explains tsgo/TypeScript 7 status honestly without presenting timelines as facts
recommends configs that stay compatible (standard tsconfig, no compiler-specific hacks)
does not block current work on future tooling
